collection: Republic of Guinea (Conakry) - Official Government Images
description: Images for use in a government e-administration application for the Republic of Guinea (West Africa, capital Conakry). NOT Guinea-Bissau, NOT Equatorial Guinea, NOT Papua New Guinea.
license_note: All images sourced from Wikimedia Commons under Creative Commons licenses or public domain. Verify license terms before official government use.

images.description: Official national flag of the Republic of Guinea. A vertical tricolor of red (left, #CE1126 - symbolizing sacrifice/labor), yellow (center, #FCD116 - symbolizing the sun/justice), and green (right, #009460 - symbolizing vegetation/solidarity). Adopted on November 10, 1958.
images.recommended_use: Primary flag asset for web application - scales perfectly at any size

images.description: PNG rasterized version of the official flag. Same vertical tricolor: red (#CE1126), yellow (#FCD116), green (#009460). Use as fallback where SVG is not supported.
images.recommended_use: PNG fallback for environments that don't support SVG

images.description: Official coat of arms (Sceau national) of the Republic of Guinea. Features a white shield with a white dove carrying a golden olive branch, over a ribbon with the national motto 'TRAVAIL, JUSTICE, SOLIDARITÉ' (Work, Justice, Solidarity). The shield is supported by a lion and an elephant, with the flag colors (red, yellow, green) as a banner below. Adopted in its current form in December 1997.
images.recommended_use: Primary coat of arms asset - scales perfectly for web headers, letterheads, and official documents

images.category: Coat of Arms / National Emblem (PNG fallback)
images.description: PNG rasterized version of the official coat of arms. Features dove with olive branch, national motto, lion and elephant supporters. Use as fallback where SVG is not supported.
images.recommended_use: PNG fallback for environments that don't support SVG

images.description: Main entrance of the Palais du Peuple (People's Palace) in Conakry, Guinea. This five-story government building houses the National Assembly (Assemblée Nationale) of Guinea. Located at the entrance of the Kaloum peninsula in central Conakry. The photo shows the main gate and entrance facade of the building.
images.attribution: Photo by Aboubacarkhoraa on Wikimedia Commons
images.recommended_use: Primary photo for the National Assembly / Parliament section of the application

images.category: Palais du Peuple - Full Building View
images.description: Full view of the Palais du Peuple (People's Palace) in Conakry, Guinea. This is the seat of the National Assembly of Guinea. The building is a five-story government structure located in central Conakry at the entrance of the Kaloum peninsula.
images.attribution: Photo by Aboubacarkhoraa on Wikimedia Commons
images.recommended_use: Alternative wider view of the National Assembly building

images.description: Fountain area at the Palais du Peuple (People's Palace) in Conakry, Guinea. Shows the ornamental fountain in front of the National Assembly building, providing context of the building's surroundings and grounds.
images.recommended_use: Contextual photo showing the grounds and approach to the National Assembly

images.description: Military parade (défilé) at the Palace in Conakry, Guinea. Shows the Guinean armed forces during an official ceremony at a government palace, with the national flag and official decorations visible. This image captures the ceremonial use of the government palace grounds.
images.recommended_use: Photo showing government ceremony/official event at the palace

images.description: Historical photo of the French Governor's Palace (Palais du Gouverneur) in Conakry, from 1956, during the colonial era. This building later became an important government building after Guinea's independence in 1958. It represents the colonial architectural heritage of Guinea's government buildings.
images.recommended_use: Historical/archival context - shows the colonial-era government palace

presidential_palace.name: Palais Présidentiel Sekhoutoureya
presidential_palace.note: No freely licensed photo found on Wikimedia Commons. The Sekhoutoureah Presidential Palace (opened 2010, Chinese-built) is the official residence of the President. Stock photos are available on Getty Images and Alamy but require licensing. Recommend contacting the Présidence de la République de Guinée (presidence.gov.gn) for an official image.

ministry_building.name: Government Ministry Building (modern)
ministry_building.note: No freely licensed photo of a modern Guinea government ministry building found on Wikimedia Commons. The Palais du Peuple photos above can serve as the primary government building image. For ministry-specific images, recommend contacting the relevant government ministry or the Service-Public (service-public.gov.gn).

flag_specifications.orientation: Vertical stripes (left to right: red, yellow, green)
